reasoning: The state of emergency (SOE) in Samoa was declared on March 31, 2025, in response to a severe energy crisis, and is officially scheduled to expire on April 29, 2025. The core uncertainty is whether the government will extend this SOE beyond its original 30-day mandate to cover April 30. Recent reporting as of April 21, 2025, indicates that the Electric Power Corporation (EPC) has officially cancelled its power rationing program. This was made possible by the arrival of temporary leased generators from New Zealand, which have successfully restored sufficient generation capacity to the grid. With the acute phase of the power shortage over and rationing cancelled, the primary political and practical justifications for maintaining emergency powers are effectively moot. Furthermore, while Samoa has recently faced a dengue fever outbreak, historical precedent and current reporting show this is being managed via a standard public health "outbreak" declaration rather than a nationwide SOE. Therefore, it is highly likely the current SOE will be allowed to lapse on April 29 as scheduled, leaving no SOE in effect on April 30.
